mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) following rate declines across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances when UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative financing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains flat, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and tightening supply chains, which compresses input costs and boosts short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units indicates a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as economic momentum stalls.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no negative bias when HOUST drops below 900.0, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio above 0.0 for the signal over a 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms wage-driven consumption expansion, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and RSAFS growth signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real household purchasing power and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with the signal information coefficient remaining below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month during economic expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2% fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, with mean returns falling below -0.5% and failing a one-sided t-test at the 5% level.
